framework: A path qualifies only when >=3 prominent, proven people in the field visibly share the capability, each with a resolving link — the pattern across standouts is the evidence, not market demand. Ranked by strength of that pattern. Curated editorial, not DOL-derived.

paths.what_it_is: Generating architectural form through code and parametric logic (Grasshopper, Rhino scripting, custom algorithms) rather than manual drafting alone.
paths.why_it_signals: It shows a designer can originate a signature formal language and push it through fabrication-ready complexity, not just operate someone else's software.

paths.path: Hand-drawing and watercolor as the core design-thinking tool
paths.what_it_is: Using freehand sketches or watercolor as the primary instrument for developing and testing a design concept before it is drafted or modeled.
paths.why_it_signals: It shows a designer can generate and interrogate an idea directly through drawing fluency, a skill juries and principals still read as evidence of design authorship.

paths.path: Material science and digital fabrication research crossover
paths.what_it_is: Running or contributing to a research practice that bridges architecture with materials science, robotics, or synthetic biology to invent new building materials and fabrication methods.
paths.why_it_signals: It shows a designer can operate past the boundary of conventional practice and produce inventions (new materials, robotic construction processes) that firms and universities compete to hire.
